ops.title: Track S gate report — synckit onto daemonkit sub-primitives (CLEAN-PROCEED, U0-U8 work order)
ops.body:
# GATE REPORT — Track S: synckit → daemonkit rewire (fusekit task 979cca8)

Scope contract: RPC bytes frozen, public API frozen, consumers (cookiesync @ synckit v0.16.0, reposync @ synckit v0.9.0) must not notice.

---

## 1. Adoption mapping: synckit mechanism → daemonkit primitive

| # | synckit mechanism | daemonkit primitive | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A1 | LF framing on Unix socket (`rpc.ReadLine`/`Encode*`, synckit/rpc/line.go:10-29, protocol.go:45-78) | `wire.NewFraming(conn)` with `MaxLine = rpc.MaxLine` (16 MiB; daemonkit default is 1 MiB — daemonkit/wire/framing.go:45) | HIGH | Unix path only; `wire.Framing` requires `net.Conn` (framing.go:33) |
| A2 | Request/Response envelope `{method,params}` / `{ok,result,error}` (synckit/rpc/protocol.go:29-43) | **KEEP synckit structs — no daemonkit primitive.** `wire.Server`'s envelope is rejected (see §2) | HIGH (keep) | Frozen bytes; `params` and `result` have no `omitempty` |
| A3 | Peer UID/PID before first byte (synckit/rpc/server.go:73-95, peercred_darwin.go:24-25) | `wire.PeerFromConn` (daemonkit/wire/peer.go:17) + `trust.Policy.Check` (trust/policy.go:85) | MEDIUM-HIGH | `PeerFromConn` lacks PeerSID — keep synckit's Darwin PID→SID plumbing (consent requestor identity, synckit/consent/service.go:32); `trust.Policy` compares `Geteuid` vs synckit's `Getuid` (policy.go:96 vs server.go:80-82) — pin with a test |
| A4 | Dispatch: `Register`/`RegisterExclusive`, 10-min timeout, panic capture (synckit/rpc/dispatch.go:24-113) | **KEEP synckit `rpc.Dispatcher`** | HIGH (keep) | Exclusive-set semantics and error text are wire-visible |
| A5 | One-request-per-connection Unix loop (synckit/rpc/server.go:49-120) | **KEEP** synckit loop; compose A1/A3 inside it | HIGH (keep) | |
| A6 | `ServeConn(io.ReadWriter)` stdio/SSH persistent stream (synckit/rpc/server.go:122-148) | **KEEP** — no daemonkit equivalent (`net.Conn` constraint) | HIGH (keep) | reposync's live service path |
| A7 | Socket singleton — currently NONE; `rpc.Listen` unconditionally unlinks (synckit/rpc/server.go:36-47) | `proc.SingleEntrant` (daemonkit/proc/singleentrant.go:15-70) | HIGH | Deliberate improvement: second `serve` gets `ErrPeerStarting` instead of stealing the socket; wire-invisible |
| A8 | `reconcile.lock` flock + state write (synckit/hostregistry/store.go:96-115,177-202) | `proc.Flock` (daemonkit/proc/flock.go:67-99) + `daemon.StateFile.UpdateUnlocked` (daemonkit/daemon/state.go:16-53) | MEDIUM-HIGH | Must keep context-bounded wait (daemonkit `StateFile.Update` alone is fail-fast) and `ErrLockBusy` identity via alias (§4) |
| A9 | XDG socket/state discovery `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/synckit/rpc.sock` (synckit/hostregistry/store.go:59-94) | **DO NOT adopt `paths.Paths`** — it emits `~/<App>/daemon.sock` and ignores XDG (daemonkit/paths/paths.go:16-38) | HIGH (keep) | Path bytes are a consumer contract |
| A10 | Shutdown: ctx cancel + watcher wait; RPC handlers untracked (synckit/daemon/root.go:20-30, serve.go:341-355) | `drain.Simple` (daemonkit/drain/simple.go:13-49) | MEDIUM | Requires net-new admission accounting on handlers; `drain.Transition` explicitly NOT needed |
| A11 | Reload (SIGHUP/RPC): in-process watcher-generation swap (synckit/daemon/serve.go:101-190) | **KEEP** — application-specific; not `daemon.Takeover` | HIGH (keep) | |
| A12 | Upgrade/version eviction — currently absent (brew upgrade leaves old process) | `version.Running/Newer` + `daemon.SkewWatch` + `daemon.Takeover` + `daemon.EnsureCurrent` + `wire/lifeproto` envelopes (daemonkit/version/version.go:117-176, daemon/skew.go:15-31, daemon/takeover.go:97-151, daemon/ensure.go:32-69, wire/lifeproto/lifeproto.go:13-83) | MEDIUM | Net-new capability; needs an `Installed`-version reader (GAP) and a `daemon.Peer` adapter; lifecycle methods are **additive** RPC methods only |
| A13 | LaunchAgent for resident `serve` (synckit/daemon/service.go:20-54) | `service.Agent` (daemonkit/service/service.go:26-105,195-229) | MEDIUM | Only `serve`; cannot express the 900s `StartInterval` reconcile job or `LimitLoadToSessionType` helpers — those stay synckit; hardcodes `ThrottleInterval=10` (defined behavior change, consumer-invisible) |
| A14 | Install EIO bootout/bootstrap retry ×6 (synckit/service/service.go:31-35,199-229) | **KEEP loop**; arithmetic via `proc.Backoff` (daemonkit/proc/backoff.go:5-24) | HIGH (keep) | `proc.Backoff` is arithmetic-only |
| A15 | Watcher/consumer/transport backoff loops (synckit/daemon/serve.go:24-43,229-303; syncservice/transport.go:200-295) | `proc.Backoff` for arithmetic; loops/healthy-reset stay synckit | HIGH | |
| A16 | Stdio/SSH child teardown (EOF → 2s → pgroup kill; synckit/syncservice/transport.go:323-360) | **KEEP** — `proc.Spawn` is for detached socket children, not framed stdio | HIGH (keep) | |
| A17 | Binary materialization (GoReleaser + Homebrew) | **GAP — no primitive; keep as-is** | HIGH (keep) | |

## 2. Hard mismatches / structural surprises

**Mismatches (each has a freeze-preserving route-around; none forces breaking the frozen surface):**

1. **`wire.Server` is byte-incompatible with the frozen reply wire** — it emits `version/rejected/reason/err/payload` and always nests handler output in `payload` (daemonkit/wire/envelope.go:20, wire/server.go:383) vs frozen flat `ok/result/error`. It also changes dispatch semantics (pools, tenant gates, reserved lifecycle ops, different trust ordering). **Resolution: `wire.Server` and `wire.ReadJSON` are excluded from scope; adopt sub-primitives only (rows A1/A3).** This is the one item that would be a MISMATCH-STOP if full `wire.Server` adoption were mandatory.
2. `wire.Framing` default 1 MiB vs the 16 MiB contract — configuration, not a stop (framing.go:45).
3. `wire.Framing` needs `net.Conn`; `ServeConn(io.ReadWriter)` keeps synckit `ReadLine` (row A6).
4. `PeerFromConn` has no PeerSID; consent identity depends on it — keep Darwin plumbing (row A3).
5. `trust.Policy` euid vs synckit uid — equivalent for the normal user daemon, divergent under setuid; pin with a test.
6. `paths.Paths` is XDG-incompatible — not adopted (row A9).
7. `service.Agent` cannot express `StartInterval`/`LimitLoadToSessionType`; partial adoption only (row A13).

**Structural surprises (gate conditions, not stops):**

- **synckit baseline is not green**: `daemon/TestReloadRPCGenerationOutlivesRequest` raced (synckit/daemon/serve_test.go:131) and two authkit tests failed during recon. The reload/generation area is inside the rewire blast radius — baseline must be green before any rewire diff lands (work-order U0).
- **`params` is never omitted** — comment at synckit/syncservice/client.go:113-120 claims empty origin "omits the key"; the implementation marshals `"params":null` (no `omitempty`, rpc/protocol.go:31-34). The consumers-lane claim "empty origins are omitted" is wrong at the byte level. Goldens must pin `null`.
- **No serialized-frame goldens exist anywhere** — the byte contract today lives only in struct tags + `json.Marshal` + framing code (rpc-bytes lane). Frame goldens are prerequisite work (U1), though per criterion 518d812 they complement, never replace, dedicated semantic tests.
- **Process note**: the consumers-lane reply file was empty on disk; its output was recovered from the run log (item_172) — paperwork failure, work completed, no re-dispatch needed.

## 3. Verified preserved-semantics list (criterion 518d812: one dedicated test per semantic)

Blast radius: HIGH = code the rewire touches; LOW = untouched unless the named migration happens.

| # | Semantic (verifier-corrected) | Lives at | Tests today | Test required post-rewire | Blast |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| S1 | 16 MiB inbound line cap; largest accepted payload is `MaxLine-1` incl. LF; outbound unbounded; consent relay **truncates** via `LimitReader`, never rejects (synckit/daemon/consent.go:210-217) | rpc/protocol.go:25-27, rpc/line.go:14-29; read sites server.go:95,135, client.go:48, bridge.go:22, syncservice/transport.go:378-386 | Only oversized-Unix-request: rpc/rpc_test.go:385-420 | Exact-boundary test (accept `MaxLine-1`+LF, reject `MaxLine`) on the Framing-backed path; `ServeConn` cap test; consent-relay truncation-preservation test | HIGH |
| S2 | Same-UID check before first byte — Unix `Serve` only; `ServeConn` has no check by design; non-Darwin fallback is `os.Getuid()` with fs perms as boundary (rpc/peercred_other.go:11-16; note `daemon/paths.go:24-33` `MkdirAll(0700)` doesn't repair a pre-existing permissive dir) | rpc/server.go:73-95 (mismatch reject at 80-82), peercred_darwin.go:24-25 | Admit-current-UID only: rpc_test.go:422-438; PID/SID plumbing: peercred_darwin_test.go:13-82 | Adversarial foreign-UID rejected-before-first-byte test (mock peer seam); euid==uid pin test for the `trust.Policy` swap; `ServeConn` no-check pin test | HIGH |
| S3 | Exit-255 scoped: one-shot SSH fails over only on unwrapped `*exec.ExitError` code 255; consent probes route around any `*SSHError`; long-lived SSHStdio binds on first response byte; relay never exits 255 | hostregistry/dial.go:19,90-103,159-168; consent/route.go:18,180-205; syncservice/transport.go:148-197,378-386; daemon/consent.go:179-225 | Complete: dial_test.go:62,82,101,300; route_test.go:202,248,342; daemon/consent_test.go:277; syncservice_test.go:378-441 | Existing dedicated tests suffice; must stay green unchanged | LOW |
| S4 | Record-before-notify anti-echo: digest recorded under mutex before fanout; **resolver** failures suppress record+notify; **gate** failures do NOT — logged, treated not-busy, evaluation proceeds (verifier-refuted; watch/engine.go:215-226, engine_test.go:578-607). Fingerprint stability is an external consumer contract, not synckit's (daemon/serve.go:357-384) | watch/engine.go:192-206; origin skip converge/converge.go:108-123; origins daemon/serve.go:455-465 | engine_test.go:286-306 does NOT prove ordering (passes even if record moved after fanout); origin tests serve_test.go:303-322, converge_test.go:258 | A true ordering proof: instrumented notifier asserting `lastDigest` already recorded when notify fires; keep gate-failure-notifies test | LOW–MED |
| S5 | Per-peer breaker — notification path only; open→suppress, 30s→5min backoff, empty-ID catch-up, self-exclusion, generation cancel, bounded Tailscale snapshot on open (daemon/breaker.go:103-107,148-159); `converge.PeerStatus` is log-dedup only, fetches still occur | daemon/breaker.go:31-159; wiring serve.go:319-338 | Comprehensive: breaker_test.go:161-365; status: converge/status_test.go:8-64 | Keep; add a two-peer independence test (verifier: current tests don't prove it) | LOW |
| S6 | Tombstones: remove wins ties; `Remove(id, 0)` is a no-op (needs strictly-newer positive stamp — verifier correction); max-join merge; persisted-not-reconciled; `Compact` may resurrect past horizon; no production synckit caller of `Compact` (cookiesync calls it at 30 days) | cregistry/cregistry.go:47-99,113-163; converge/converge.go:103-132 | Rich: cregistry_test.go:145-207,361-405,407-601; converge_test.go:144,283 | Keep; serialization coverage exists at cregistry_test.go:535-601 | LOW |
| S7 | Monotone CRDT stamps: direct equal-stamp `Add` is **first-wins, order-dependent** (cregistry.go:80-86); canonical-JSON tie-break happens only through `Merge` (can panic on non-serializable payload, cregistry.go:182-190); `SaveRegistry` runs every pass; physical writes are atomic, not monotone | cregistry.go:76-99,135-190; converge.go:123-128; hostregistry/store.go:177-202 | cregistry_test.go:74-141,277-359,703 (duplicate delivery — NOT converge_test.go:699, which doesn't exist) | Keep; add physical-write idempotence characterization only if converge is touched | LOW |
| S8 | Canceled **cold** refresh not cached — parent-ctx gate before stamping; but internal 5s child timeout with live parent IS cached, and a canceled caller can receive a warm cache (verifier: original claim too broad); `TrustedPeer/Origin` use `context.Background()` | meshtrust/trust.go:18-20,127-153 | Pre-canceled cold-cache only: trust_test.go:171-187; TTL/failure/single-flight: :85-242 | Only if meshtrust is touched (it isn't in this plan): in-flight-cancel and internal-timeout-cached tests | LOW |
| S9 | Disconnect cancels handler ctx — Unix `Serve` raw-conn watcher (server.go:108-118); `ServeConn` has NO mid-handler EOF cancellation (outer ctx only); 10-min dispatch timeout composes | rpc/server.go:102-119; dispatch.go:72-90 | Unix: rpc_test.go:320-357; timeout: :284; nothing for `ServeConn` | Re-run Unix test on Framing-backed server; new `ServeConn` pin tests: outer-ctx cancellation propagates, mid-handler EOF does NOT cancel | HIGH |

## 4. Consumer-breakage risks and the frozen-surface guarantees that neutralize them

Consumers pin old versions (cookiesync `go.mod:13` → v0.16.0; reposync `go.mod:10` → v0.9.0), so compile-time surface is untouched by the rewire; the live contracts are wire bytes, filesystem, CLI, manifest, and behavior between the **new synckitd** and **old-pinned consumer binaries**.

| Risk | Frozen guarantee |
|---|---|
| Frame bytes drift (field names/order, `"params":null`, `"result":null` on errors, LF framing, no length prefix) | Keep synckit `rpc.Request`/`Response` structs verbatim (A2); U1 frame goldens pin exact bytes incl. `params:null` |
| `unknown method %q` text changes — reposync **string-matches** `"unknown method"` (reposync internal/reconcile/env.go:74-75) | Keep synckit `Dispatcher` (A4); golden pins the exact string |
| Timeout constants — cookiesync uses `DispatchTimeout - 30s` twice | `ReadTimeout=30s`, `DispatchTimeout=10m` unchanged (rpc/protocol.go:16-23) |
| One-shot Unix vs persistent `ServeConn` semantics; `rpc.Proxy` byte-exact forwarding (int64 CRDT stamps) | A5/A6 keep both loops; bridge untouched (rpc/bridge.go:11-41) |
| `PeerPID`/`PeerSID` loss → cookiesync auth identity (`sid:<id>`, cookiesync internal/daemon/grants.go:10-22) breaks | Keep Darwin PID→SID plumbing (A3 carve-out) |
| Path drift: `~/.config/synckit/{state.json,rpc.sock,reconcile.lock,manifests/}`, XDG resolution, per-tool `rpc.sock` | daemonkit `paths` not adopted (A9) |
| CLI surface: `synckitd install`/`host ls`/`host add`/`consent relay`/`consent presence`; names `synckitd`, mesh `synckit`; label `com.github.yasyf.synckit.helper.cookiesync` | No command or naming changes in scope |
| Manifest schema: must keep accepting reposync's v0.9-shaped manifest (`Watch.Backend:"fsnotify"`, `stdio`, `ServeArgs:["rpc-serve"]`) and cookiesync's v0.16 socket/helper shape | Manifest package untouched; U8 interop check exercises both |
| ProtocolVersion skew: daemon fatally rejects ≠1 (synckit/daemon/reconcile.go:63-81) | `ProtocolVersion=1` and the five `svc.*` methods frozen; lifecycle endpoints (A12) are additive new methods only |
| SSH behavior: `.local`-then-FQDN order, dedupe, 255 classification, `SSHError` fields+`Unwrap` (cookiesync `errors.As` at internal/daemon/union.go:35-38) | hostregistry dial/transport untouched (S3 stays) |
| Error identity: typed `consent.{AuthRequired,BindingMismatch,Denied}`, `Classify`, `ProbeRoutesAround`, `authkit.HelperError`, `*rpc.TransportError` | Types not moved; string-equivalence explicitly insufficient |

**Sentinel-aliasing list (the only permitted sentinel changes; per STYLEGUIDE a moved sentinel lives in exactly one package):**

- `hostregistry.ErrLockBusy = proc.ErrLockBusy` (daemonkit/proc/errors.go:27) — replaces synckit's re-declaration (synckit/hostregistry/store.go:36); `WithLock` keeps `%w`-wrapping it so reposync's `errors.Is(err, state.ErrLockBusy)` (reposync internal/tui/repos.go:394 via internal/state/state.go:47-48) keeps holding.
- `rpc.ErrFrameTooLarge = wire.ErrFrameTooLarge`, `rpc.ErrFrameContainsLF = wire.ErrFrameContainsLF` (daemonkit/wire/framing.go:21,25) — new, rpc-internal.
- Only if trust failures become locally exposed: alias `trust.ErrUntrustedPeer` / `trust.ErrNoVerifier` (daemonkit/trust/policy.go:19,22).
- **Never** alias `wire.ErrUntrustedPeer` (belongs to the rejected `wire.Server` path) or ladder sentinels.

## 5. Corrections the verifier forced on the enumerations

1. **S4 refuted in part**: gate failures do NOT suppress record/notify — logged, treated not-busy, notification proceeds (watch/engine.go:215-226; engine_test.go:578-607 expects one notification). Only resolver failures suppress.
2. **S4 test claim refuted**: `TestAntiEchoSameHashTerminatesLoop` (engine_test.go:286-306) is not an ordering proof — it would pass with record-after-fanout.
3. **S4**: fingerprint stability is a consumer-supplied external contract (daemon/serve.go:357-384), not established by synckit.
4. **S6**: `Remove(id, 0)` creates nothing — absent-item tombstones need a positive/newer stamp (cregistry.go:94-98).
5. **S7**: direct equal-stamp `Add` is first-wins and order-dependent (cregistry.go:80-86); canonical tie-break exists only in `Merge`; `Merge` canonicalization can panic on non-JSON-serializable payloads (cregistry.go:182-190).
6. **S7 cite fixed**: `converge/converge_test.go:699` does not exist (file is 407 lines); duplicate-delivery coverage is `cregistry/cregistry_test.go:703`.
7. **S8 narrowed**: "canceled refresh never cached" is too broad — internal 5s child-timeout expiry with a live parent IS cached; a canceled caller can be served a warm cache (trust.go:135-153).
8. **Stale cites corrected**: UID reject `rpc/server.go:80-82` (not 78); `LOCAL_PEERCRED` `peercred_darwin.go:24-25`; constants `hostregistry/dial.go:19`, `consent/route.go:18`; cancel watcher `rpc/server.go:108-109,115-118`; child reset `syncservice/transport.go:170-174,297-310`.
9. **Consumers**: cookiesync imports 12 synckit packages / reposync 8; reposync does NOT import `watch`, `daemon`, `meshtrust`, or `consent`; `env.get_state` is not a syncservice method and is not capability-advertised; reposync unknown-method compat is string-based, not a sentinel; cookiesync has NO production `errors.Is` against a synckit sentinel (its typed matches are `errors.As`); cookiesync RPC errors lose type identity crossing `resp.Error`; reposync's live path is `ServeConn` over stdio (its `state.SockPath` is unused).
10. **Cross-lane byte correction**: the "empty origin omits params" comment is false — the wire carries `"params":null` (rpc-bytes lane, syncservice/client.go:113-120).
11. **Process**: Enumeration B was never diffed row-by-row (verifier produced an independent baseline + contradiction list); a literal row-diff needs a follow-up call with B's full text. Consumers-lane reply file was empty; output recovered from the run log — completed work, no re-dispatch.

## 6. Recommended implementation work-order (bounded units)

| Unit | Work | Files to touch | Verifying test |
|---|---|---|---|
| **U0 — Baseline green** (blocks all) | Fix/deflake `TestReloadRPCGenerationOutlivesRequest` race; triage the two authkit failures | synckit/daemon/serve_test.go:131 (± daemon/serve.go); authkit tests | synckit `task test` green across repeated runs |
| **U1 — Semantic pins + frame goldens** (on unmodified synckit, before any structural change) | Author the missing dedicated tests from §3 (S1 boundary/ServeConn/relay-truncation, S2 foreign-UID + euid pin + ServeConn no-check, S4 ordering proof, S9 ServeConn cancellation pins) plus full-frame byte goldens incl. `"params":null` and `unknown method %q` | synckit/rpc/rpc_test.go, watch/engine_test.go, syncservice tests, new golden test file | All pass on current synckit (characterization) |
| **U2 — Dep wiring** | Add daemonkit to synckit go.mod; untracked `go.work` for dev (never a committed `replace`) | synckit/go.mod | `task build` both repos |
| **U3 — Unix server core swap** | `wire.NewFraming` (MaxLine=16 MiB) + `wire.PeerFromConn` + `trust.Policy.Check` pre-read inside synckit's existing accept loop; keep Request/Response, Dispatcher, ServeConn, PID→SID; alias frame sentinels | synckit/rpc/server.go, rpc/client.go; rpc/line.go stays (ServeConn) | U1 pins + full rpc suite + goldens byte-identical |
| **U4 — proc adoption** | `proc.SingleEntrant` replaces unconditional socket unlink in `Listen` (documented improvement); hostregistry flock → `proc.Flock`; `ErrLockBusy = proc.ErrLockBusy` alias with `%w` wrap preserved | synckit/rpc/server.go:36-47, hostregistry/store.go | hostregistry suite + new `errors.Is(WithLock err, ErrLockBusy)` alias test + duel-listener eviction test |
| **U5 — drain.Simple** | Admission accounting on RPC handlers; shutdown settles in-flight, refuses new | synckit/rpc/server.go, daemon/serve.go | New drain test: in-flight handler completes, post-drain conn refused; existing shutdown tests green |
| **U6 — version/skew/takeover** (separable phase 2) | `version.Running/Newer`, `SkewWatch` + new Installed-version reader, `Takeover`, lifeproto endpoints as additive methods | synckit/daemon/serve.go, new daemon/version glue | New lifecycle tests + goldens regression proving existing method bytes unchanged |
| **U7 — service.Agent (serve only)** | `service.Agent` for resident serve; keep synckit AgentSpec for StartInterval reconcile + session-limited helpers; keep EIO retry loop with `proc.Backoff` arithmetic | synckit/service/service.go, daemon/service.go, daemon/install.go | Plist golden diff — only the deliberate `ThrottleInterval=10` delta; install retry test |
| **U8 — Consumer interop gate** | New synckitd vs old-pinned consumer binaries (cookiesync @ f1cb768, reposync @ 46b0fb9): `svc.*` round-trips, `env.get_state` passthrough, stdio spawn per v0.9 manifest, `synckitd host ls`, consent relay/presence | none (harness only) | Interop matrix green; both consumer repos still build untouched |

U1 is the criterion-518d812 gate: no structural unit (U3+) lands before its semantic's dedicated test exists.

## 7. Verdict

**CLEAN-PROCEED**, with a scoped adoption boundary and two preconditions.

Rationale: the single genuine wire-level incompatibility — `wire.Server`'s `version/rejected/reason/err/payload` envelope vs the frozen `ok/result/error` contract — is fully neutralized by the daemonkit-fit lane's sub-primitive strategy (framing + peer + trust adopted; envelope, dispatcher, and connection loops kept), which two lanes independently converged on and the verifier did not contradict. Every other mismatch (1 MiB default, `net.Conn` constraint, missing PeerSID, euid/uid, XDG paths, launchd expressiveness) has a config knob or an explicit keep. Consumer freeze holds because both consumers pin old module versions and every live contract (bytes, paths, CLI names, manifest shapes, sentinel/type identity) stays in untouched or byte-pinned code, with `ErrLockBusy` the sole sentinel move — handled by aliasing, not re-declaration.

Conditions: (a) `wire.Server`/`wire.ReadJSON` and daemonkit `paths` are explicitly out of scope — any lane that drifts toward them re-opens this gate; (b) U0 baseline-green and U1 dedicated-semantic-tests land before any structural diff; (c) lifecycle additions (U6) are additive methods only, gated by the frame goldens.
ops.when: when implementing, reviewing, or scoping Track S (synckit rewire onto daemonkit primitives); the frozen-wire adoption boundary, preserved-semantics test list, sentinel-alias list, and unit work-order
